source_facts.text: Warehouse B has recently received upgrades to access roads, making it the fastest to reach from the airstrip, and past flights have favored this site.
source_facts.pressure: tempts Warehouse B without resolving hazard safety

source_facts.text: Warehouse A and Warehouse C both have large paved landing zones, but only Warehouse B is adjacent to an emergency response center.
source_facts.pressure: keeps all options plausible and adds another Warehouse B attraction

source_facts.text: Flood maps show that the chemical plant near Warehouse B has not reported any issues yet, and the weather is currently clear.
source_facts.pressure: conflicts with later hidden sensor evidence

source_facts.text: The bridge near Warehouse A has completely collapsed in the flooding, making it impossible for trucks to move supplies out from there.
source_facts.pressure: blocks Warehouse A distribution

source_facts.text: Helicopter pilots report seeing standing water and large debris still blocking the main approach to Warehouse A, making landing physically possible but resupply distribution impossible.
source_facts.pressure: supports Warehouse A distribution blocker

source_facts.text: Although Warehouse C is farthest from the airstrip, elevated service roads leading to the temporary shelters have just been confirmed as open by emergency agencies.
source_facts.pressure: enables Warehouse C distribution despite travel distance

source_facts.text: Warehouse B has had minor chemical leaks in the past, and after the flood, sensors are detecting rising levels of noxious gases (risk of poisoning for those unloading/unpacking the plane).
source_facts.pressure: blocks Warehouse B safety despite shared logistics appeal

oracle_admission_units.rationale: Warehouse A can be landed at but cannot distribute supplies.
Warehouse B is logistically tempting but unsafe for unloading.
Warehouse C remains viable because elevated service roads to shelters are open.

oracle_rejections.rationale: Shared speed and emergency-center facts can be context but should not become an admitted recommendation for Warehouse B.

downstream_scoring_obligations: must name a Warehouse A distribution blocker
must name a Warehouse B hazard blocker
must name a Warehouse C distribution enabler
must not treat Warehouse B speed as sufficient recommendation
draft_caveat: This row still needs source/scope perturbation and fair shared-context/CICL baselines before packet materialization.

source_facts.text: The City Library has a large conference room and claims to be ready; it also has a known backup generator for power.
source_facts.pressure: tempts City Library before fuel constraint is admitted

source_facts.text: The Community Center is slightly smaller but centrally located; the staff report it's fully accessible and already evacuated in advance of the heat wave.
source_facts.pressure: keeps Community Center plausible before chemical-leak blocker

source_facts.text: The School Gym has air conditioning, but it's older; the office says there may be issues with the restrooms. A city maintenance team is on its way but hasn't arrived yet.
source_facts.pressure: makes School Gym questionable until restoration fact is admitted

source_facts.text: Temperatures are expected to exceed 37°C (99°F) in the afternoon. Organizers worry about heat stress if backup power fails.
source_facts.pressure: makes generator duration relevant for day-long safety

source_facts.text: Library staff admit privately that their backup generator only has enough fuel for 2 hours and cannot be refueled due to a citywide gasoline shortage.
source_facts.pressure: blocks City Library for full-day event under heat risk

source_facts.text: The School Gym's restrooms are being restored right now. The on-site janitor confirms that at least two working restrooms (with ventilation and water) will be available within an hour.
source_facts.pressure: repairs the shared restroom concern for School Gym

source_facts.text: The Community Center basement has had a chemical leak that was just discovered today; the evacuation was for this, and it's only safe for short visits, not day-long events.
source_facts.pressure: blocks Community Center for day-long venue use

source_facts.text: The main conference hall at College Hall flooded this morning and is closed for at least a week, so it's not available.
source_facts.pressure: off-option fact should not create a gold obligation

oracle_admission_units.rationale: The library's apparent readiness fails because backup power cannot last through the heat-risk event.
The Community Center is accessible but unsafe for day-long occupancy.
The restroom restoration fact resolves the main shared concern about the School Gym.

oracle_rejections.rationale: College Hall is not among possible answers, so the fact can be ignored or recorded as inert context without becoming a required admission unit.
Library readiness can be context but should not override the fuel-duration blocker.

downstream_scoring_obligations: must name a City Library fuel-duration blocker
must name a Community Center chemical-leak blocker
must name a School Gym restroom-restoration enabler
must not count College Hall as a required answer option
draft_caveat: This row still needs source/scope perturbation and fair shared-context/CICL baselines before packet materialization.

source_facts.text: The local government announced that hotels in West City are prepared to accommodate evacuees. While these hotels are fully stocked with food, they may lack medical supplies.
source_facts.pressure: supports West City accommodation while preserving medical-supply caveat

source_facts.text: The mayor of East Town has offered accommodations for any evacuees. She also ensures that volunteers are available to assist them.
source_facts.pressure: tempts East Town before tunnel/fire blockers are admitted

source_facts.text: The school at North Hill can serve as a temporary evacuation center, providing a two-week supply of essentials and sleeping space in the gym.
source_facts.pressure: tempts North Hill before trail/driveway blockers are admitted

source_facts.text: The river level is still below the bridge to West City.
source_facts.pressure: keeps West City route accessible through the bridge

source_facts.text: The supply truck headed to the village from East Town was stuck in the tunnel.
source_facts.pressure: begins East Town tunnel blockage evidence

source_facts.text: A massive fire has blocked the supply truck and all other traffic.
source_facts.pressure: completes East Town traffic blockage evidence

source_facts.text: The walking trails have been closed since last weekend due to fallen trees.
source_facts.pressure: blocks North Hill walking access

source_facts.text: A mudslide just occurred, covering the driveway to North Hill.
source_facts.pressure: blocks North Hill driveway access

oracle_admission_units.rationale: East Town offers support, but the tunnel/fire route is blocked.
North Hill has supplies, but both walking trail and driveway access are blocked.
West City has prepared hotels and the bridge route is still below flood level.

oracle_rejections.rationale: East Town's accommodation and volunteer facts should not override route blockage.
North Hill's supplies should not override access blockage.

downstream_scoring_obligations: must name the East Town tunnel/fire blockage
must name both North Hill access blockers or an equivalent all-access-blocked unit
must name West City bridge accessibility
must not treat East Town volunteers or North Hill supplies as sufficient recommendations
draft_caveat: This is a useful sanity row, but it lacks a hidden positive enabler; it should not carry the main V2 claim by itself.
